arena_gate_synthesis.method_note: Both source reports were read directly from their exact commits via git show, not from branch tips. Every carried-forward finding was independently re-verified against the working tree at base_commit (grep/read of the actual code), not merely copied. Two source claims did not survive verification and are corrected below. No audit branch was merged into main; no production code or Markdown was modified by this synthesis.

owner_correction_notice.ruling: Direct owner ruling REVERSES this document's original access classification. Current production access for Arena Hall, Battle Room, and Arena Master Console must be STAFF_OR_SUPERUSER_ONLY; public/registered-author access is NOT_RELEASED.
owner_correction_notice.superseded_evidence: RELEASE_JOURNAL v2.5.380 ('Any registered author can visit the arena') is classified HISTORICAL_SUPERSEDED_RULE and MAY NOT override current owner policy.
what_changed_in_this_document: The original AR1 (which called registered-author access an intended owner decision and the product contract the 'stale' side) is WITHDRAWN. It was wrong.
Registered-author access to the Arena is now classified as a CONFIRMED CURRENT DEFECT (new finding CF0).
The product contract (CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md:123-138, recipe_author_without_staff: false) is AUTHORITATIVE AND CORRECT — it is NOT marked stale.
Conflicting-evidence CE2 resolution is flipped: current owner policy + the canonical contract win; the code is the defect.
The permission implementation package and targeted-test matrix are updated to fix the over-exposure and to invert the tests that currently assert the defective behaviour.
unchanged_by_this_correction: CF3 / CE1 correction: battle_chat_send IS protected (@chef_battle_guard); battle_chat_poll needs separate assessment.
CF2 arena_react has no gate; CF4/CF5 accessibility; CF6 stale test name.

deployment_journal.data_hygiene_note: RELEASE_JOURNAL entries 2.5.376 through 2.5.380 all carry commit="pending" — their real commit hashes were never backfilled. Not fixed by this synthesis (Markdown/journal edits out of scope); flagged so a future owner-approved task records them.

arena_access_permission_matrix.owner_required_policy: Arena Hall, Battle Room, all their polling endpoints, and all privileged actions: STAFF_OR_SUPERUSER_ONLY during dark launch. Anonymous and registered-author-without-staff must be denied. Master Console: staff/superuser-only maximum (currently stricter — superuser+owner/flag — which is within bound).

current_code_behaviour_dark_launch.note: CHEF_BATTLE_ENABLED=False (dark launch). Rows marked DEFECT are wider than owner policy allows.

master_console + master/* + live-arena/*.verdict: WITHIN BOUND — stricter than staff/superuser, not over-exposed

arena_access_permission_matrix.root_cause: chef_battle/access.py:43-44 — is_battle_visible() returns True for any user with a recipe_author_profile during dark launch. That single branch is what lets non-staff authors into everything gated by chef_battle_guard/is_battle_visible (Hall, Battle Room, arena_ping/state/blast, battle_state_poll, battle_chat_send). arena_react (no gate) and battle_chat_poll (ratelimit only) are separately even wider (anonymous).

confirmed_findings.origin: OWNER RULING (reverses original AR1) + backend F1 evidence
confirmed_findings.title: Production Arena is accessible wider than owner policy allows: registered non-staff authors can reach Arena Hall + Battle Room during dark launch
confirmed_findings.verified: YES — chef_battle/access.py:36-44. During dark launch (CHEF_BATTLE_ENABLED=False), is_battle_visible() returns True for any account holding a recipe_author_profile (lines 43-44), so a plain registered author with no staff flag reaches the Arena Hall, Battle Room, arena_ping/state/blast, battle_state_poll, and battle_chat_send. Owner policy requires staff/superuser-only pre-release. This is exactly the release-gate defect the product contract's own escalation rule (CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md section 5) describes.
confirmed_findings.policy_source: Direct owner ruling 2026-07-21 (staff/superuser-only, not released) + CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md:123-138 (recipe_author_without_staff: false). The v2.5.380 journal rule to the contrary is historical/superseded and does not apply.
confirmed_findings.contract_status: The product contract is AUTHORITATIVE AND CORRECT on this point. It must NOT be marked stale.
confirmed_findings.fix_side: Backend (Bolt): remove the recipe_author_profile branch from is_battle_visible() so that during dark launch only is_staff/is_superuser pass; update the docstring that cites the superseded 2026-07-20 rule. Then invert the tests that currently assert an author gets 200 (see targeted_test_matrix).
confirmed_findings.related: CF2 (arena_react, anonymous) and CF3 (battle_chat_poll, anonymous) are additional, separate over-exposure gaps beyond the author-access root cause.

confirmed_findings.title: Arena Hall initial-render context defect: crown_streak / crown_ladder / recent_gifts always empty on first paint
confirmed_findings.verified: YES — re-confirmed at base_commit. templates/chef_battle/arena.html reads {{ crown_streak|default:0 }} (line 58), {% for chef in crown_ladder %} (line 127), {% for gift in recent_gifts %} (line 143) as TOP-LEVEL variables. chef_battle/views.py arena() render() call (lines 1135-1142) passes only: rank_groups, spectator_count, active_battle, arena_data, viewer_author, user_enrolled. The three values exist only nested inside arena_data. Django silently resolves the undefined top-level names to empty, so crown streak renders 0 and both loops hit their {% empty %} branch on every initial load until the first arena_state poll repaints via JS.
confirmed_findings.not_tracked_in: Not mentioned in ARENA_BUILD_STAGES or RELEASE_JOURNAL — a genuinely new, un-tracked defect.
confirmed_findings.recommended_fix_side: View-side (Bolt): add crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts to the top-level render context, matching what arena.html already expects. Chosen over editing the template so backend/Python stays in Bolt's lane and arena.html (GB build-stage territory) is not touched.

confirmed_findings.title: arena_react has no visibility gate
confirmed_findings.verified: YES — chef_battle/views.py:3158-3159 shows @require_POST as the only decorator on arena_react; no @chef_battle_guard and no is_battle_visible() check in the body. Docstring says 'Public but rate-limited'. During dark launch an anonymous user can POST reactions for any battle_id while the arena page itself 404s them.
confirmed_findings.action: Under the owner's staff/superuser-only policy this must be gated with is_battle_visible()/@chef_battle_guard like its sibling arena_* endpoints. Fix is one decorator/guard.

confirmed_findings.title: battle_chat_poll has no visibility gate (read-only chat leak); battle_chat_send IS gated
confirmed_findings.verified: CONFIRMED/CORRECTED — battle_chat_poll (chef_battle/views.py:2055-2056) carries only @ratelimit(GET), no @chef_battle_guard/@login_required, so an anonymous visitor can poll and read chat for any battle id even while battle_detail 404s them. battle_chat_send (views.py:2017-2020) DOES carry @chef_battle_guard + @require_POST + @ratelimit — contrary to the backend audit's claim that it had 'no gate'. battle_chat_send is PROTECTED; only battle_chat_poll needs separate assessment.
confirmed_findings.action: Assess battle_chat_poll separately: under staff/superuser-only policy it should inherit @chef_battle_guard to match the page it serves.

confirmed_findings.title: arena_battle_popup.html live-battle popup lacks modal accessibility
confirmed_findings.verified: YES — templates/chef_battle/arena_battle_popup.html has 0 aria-/role/tabindex attributes. static/js/arena_battle_room.js:198 implements Escape-to-close, but grep found no focus trap, no initial focus entry, no aria-modal, and no focus-return-on-close. TECHNICAL_STANDARDS.md section 6 requires all of focus entry, focus trap, Escape, close controls, and focus return.
confirmed_findings.caveat: Static-evidence only (no live screen-reader run); the deficiency in the source markup is nonetheless direct and confirmed.

confirmed_findings.title: battle_detail.html has markedly lower accessibility instrumentation
confirmed_findings.verified: YES — templates/chef_battle/battle_detail.html has 4 aria-/role/tabindex occurrences vs 20 in arena.html and 21 in arena_master_console.html. A closer manual pass is warranted before Battle Room is called acceptance-ready.
confirmed_findings.caveat: A raw attribute count is a proxy, not a WCAG verdict; treat as a flag to review, not a proven barrier.

confirmed_findings.title: Stale test name test_procedural_arena_is_explicit_proto_gate
confirmed_findings.verified: YES — no ?proto=1 gate exists in chef_battle/views.py (grep for 'proto' returns nothing). The test at chef_battle/tests.py (~line 2395) passes only because the ring content now renders unconditionally; the name and any docs implying a proto gate are stale.

already_resolved_findings.title: Raw hex colours across arena stylesheets
already_resolved_findings.resolution: IN PROGRESS, ALREADY TRACKED as ARENA_BUILD_STAGES stage 6 ('tokens', GB-owned, frontend_done=false). Live raw-hex count has been driven 89 -> ~35 (recount at base_commit: arena.css 6, arena_hall.css 3, arena_render.css 3, arena_command_deck.css 0, arena_deck_polish.css 5, arena_master_console.css 8, arena_master_console_plan.css 17, arena_effects.css 0 — counts include comment and local-declaration matches). Remaining are documented semantic exceptions (green=online / red=LIVE / blue=DEF, no such hue token exists) plus each file's own local palette-declaration site.
already_resolved_findings.residual_action: Finish stage 6 to its stated criterion (grep hex == 0 except documented exceptions) and get the open owner ruling on the independent dark palette (see conflicting_evidence CE3). Rolled into greenbear_work_package, not re-opened as a new finding.

withdrawn_findings.was: Originally classified registered-author arena access as an intended owner decision (per journal v2.5.380) and the product contract as the 'stale' side.
withdrawn_findings.withdrawn_reason: Reversed by direct owner ruling 2026-07-21: staff/superuser-only is the current policy; the v2.5.380 rule is historical/superseded and cannot widen access. Registered-author access is a CONFIRMED CURRENT DEFECT (now CF0). The product contract is authoritative and correct — not stale.

conflicting_evidence.backend_audit_claim: battle_chat_send (views.py:2020) gate: NONE; anonymous can POST as 'Anonymous'.
conflicting_evidence.verified_reality: battle_chat_send carries @chef_battle_guard (views.py:2017) at base_commit — it IS gated. The backend audit's F3 overstated the gap. Only battle_chat_poll is ungated (read-only). Resolved in favour of the code; CF3 reflects the corrected, narrower finding.
conflicting_evidence.resolution: Code wins. Synthesis carries the corrected version. battle_chat_send PROTECTED; battle_chat_poll needs separate assessment.

conflicting_evidence.topic: author-without-staff arena visibility: current owner policy vs superseded journal entry
conflicting_evidence.superseded_journal_says: RELEASE_JOURNAL v2.5.380 + access.py:43-44 comment (2026-07-20) — any registered author may visit.
conflicting_evidence.resolution: FLIPPED by owner correction. Current owner ruling (staff/superuser-only, not released) and the canonical contract WIN. The v2.5.380 journal entry is HISTORICAL_SUPERSEDED_RULE and may not override current policy. The CODE (access.py author branch) is the defect (CF0). The contract is NOT stale.

conflicting_evidence.topic: canonical Arena palette: warm parchment (base.css tokens) vs independent dark near-black palette
conflicting_evidence.evidence: frontend audit + recount confirm arena_hall.css / arena_master_console.css define their own dark palette (#07090a-#1d2819 range) distinct from base.css warm-parchment light tokens. AGENTS.md section 11 / TECHNICAL_STANDARDS.md section 7 warn against an independent dark Arena token system, but the arena has visibly been built dark and the owner has been iterating on that dark hall live (build stages, journal).
conflicting_evidence.resolution: UNRESOLVED — needs an explicit owner ruling on which palette is canonical for the public 2D Hall before stage-6 token migration can pick target tokens. Blocks the palette half of greenbear_work_package.

implementation_order: 0. OWNER DECISIONS that gate parts of the work: (a) canonical palette ruling parchment-vs-dark [CE3]; (b) navigation contract full-page-vs-popup [frontend F4]. NOTE: the author-visibility question is NO LONGER an open decision — the owner has ruled staff/superuser-only, so CF0 is a fix task, not a pending decision.

1. BOLT — CF0 access fix (release-gate; restore staff/superuser-only in is_battle_visible; gate arena_react [CF2] and assess battle_chat_poll [CF3]) + invert the tests that currently assert author access. Highest priority — this is the production over-exposure.
2. BOLT — CF1 initial-render context fix + regression test + CF6 stale-test rename (independent, no owner decision needed).
3. GREENBEAR — CF4 popup accessibility (no owner decision needed) first; then stage-6 token completion after owner decision (a); then CF5 battle_detail a11y pass.

4. EMBER — live-browser + targeted-test verification of the above once identity=ember is restored; must include an explicit check that a plain author gets 404 on Hall/Battle Room after CF0 lands. Pending identity restoration.
No package merges to main without owner instruction; no deploys.

targeted_test_matrix.no_overlap_rule: Bolt runs its focused subset during development; Ember runs the broader arena access/contract set once as the linode shard at the integration gate. The full ~1500-suite is NOT run three times.
targeted_test_matrix.baseline_evidence: Bolt already ran the 7 arena access/contract classes at base_commit this session: 77 tests, 77 passed, 0 failed, 251.8s (local SQLite dev). NOTE: that green baseline includes test_plain_author_sees_the_arena_during_dark_launch, which asserts the DEFECTIVE behaviour and must be inverted by CF0 — after the fix that specific test flips, which is expected, not a regression.

deployment_journal_requirements.applies_when: Only at each future owner-authorised deploy of one of these packages — NOT part of this synthesis (no deploy performed).
per_deploy_checklist: Prepend a RELEASE_JOURNAL entry in config/release_journal.py (title, date, section 'Chef Battles', summary) — and backfill the real commit hash (do not leave commit="pending" as entries 2.5.376-380 currently are). The CF0 deploy entry should record that Arena access was returned to staff/superuser-only, explicitly superseding the v2.5.380 rule.
Bump the patch version in templates/base.html footer before the deploy.
After release, hand-sync the relevant ARENA_BUILD_STAGES done-flags in recipes/views.py (e.g. stage 6 frontend_done once tokens complete) — the board does not auto-detect completion.

Deploy via the approved non-root deploy account and the repository-approved procedure; run collectstatic --no-input for any CSS/JS change (GreenBear/Ember packages); verify the live hashed file with curl, since 'RESTARTED' does not prove new static shipped.
Every production-affecting package carries a rollback note (git revert path; no migrations expected in CF0/CF1/CF2/CF3/CF4/CF5).
deployment_journal_requirements.migration_note: None of CF0-CF6 requires a database migration (visibility branch removal, context wiring, decorators, template attributes, CSS tokens, test rename). Confirm before claiming migration-free at deploy time.
deployment_journal_requirements.release_gate_priority: CF0 is a production over-exposure defect. It should be the first package deployed once fixed and tested, ahead of the cosmetic/accessibility packages.

blockers: Owner decisions (a) canonical palette and (b) navigation contract gate the stage-6 palette half and deeper Battle Room work only. CF0, CF1, CF2, CF3, CF4, CF5, CF6 do NOT need any pending decision — the access policy is now ruled.
Ember work package is blocked pending identity=ember restoration.
This synthesis authorises no implementation, merge, or deploy — it waits on owner go-ahead to open the impl/* branches.
